{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first burial mounds to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Burial Mounds in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Tumulus Groups ! Tumulus Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior burial mound with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior burial mounds in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of burial mounds located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior burial mound, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji ware

{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first burial mounds to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Burial Mounds in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Tumulus Groups ! Tumulus Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior burial mound with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior burial mounds in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of burial mounds located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior burial mound, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar

{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first burial mounds to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Tumulus Groups ! Tumulus Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior burial mound with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior burial mounds in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of burial mounds located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior burial mound, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar

Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji ware

Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}}== See Also == * {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=draft}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳|12=draft}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=draft}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=draft}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|12=draft}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=draft}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=draft}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|12=draft}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=draft}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft|12=draft}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 下侍塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後方部） |別名 = 車塚 |所属 = |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[大田原市]]湯津上 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 46.75 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 7|経度秒 = 22.35 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長84m
高さ9.4m（後方部） |埋葬施設 = [[粘土槨]] |出土品 = 銅鏡・鉄製品・土師器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「[[侍塚古墳]]」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後方墳-南 |ラベル = 下侍塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''下侍塚古墳'''（しもさむらいづかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[大田原市]]湯津上（ゆづかみ）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[上侍塚古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」）。 上侍塚古墳とともに[[江戸時代]]に[[徳川光圀]]（[[水戸黄門]]）の命で日本で最初の学術的発掘調査が行われた古墳として知られる。本項では、下侍塚古墳の北西にある'''[[#侍塚古墳群|侍塚古墳群]]'''（大田原市指定史跡）についても解説する。 == 概要 ==
