新宿||温泉
神社||[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||[[那須八幡塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された古墳である。[[江戸時代]]の[[元禄]]5年（[[1692年]]）の発掘調査のほか、[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘長は84メートルを測り、那須地方の前方後方墳6基のうちでは上侍塚古墳に次ぐ規模になる。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められる{{Sfn|下侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされ、周濠内からは底部穿孔の土師器（赤彩の有段口辺壺）が検出されている{{Sfn|下侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における木棺の直葬で、両小口には粘土塊が置かれる{{Sfn|下侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。副葬品として、江戸期の調査では銅鏡（二神二獣鏡1）・鉄製品（刀片15・甲冑片・大刀把頭1・鉄鉾身1）・土師器（高坏4・壺か1）が検出されている{{Sfn|下侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末葉頃と推定される。上侍塚古墳とともに那須地方を代表する古墳の1つであるとともに、江戸期の調査後には上侍塚古墳と合わせて墳丘保存のための松が植えられていることから、日本で最初の学術的発掘調査かつ調査後の文化財保護の点で考古学史上においても重要視される古墳である。 古墳域は[[1951年]]（昭和26年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]、[[元禄]]5年（[[1692年]]）に[[徳川光圀]]の命で大金重貞による発掘調査。副葬品多数出土（『湯津神村車塚御修理』として記録、調査後は松板箱に入れて埋戻し）。 * [[1951年]]（昭和26年）6月9日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」のうち）。 * [[1975年]]（昭和50年）、周溝の範囲確認調査（湯津上村教育委員会、[[1976年]]に概報刊行）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}{{small|左に前方部、右に後方部。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り下侍塚古墳説明板。[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ 侍塚古墳]（大田原市ホームページ）。。 * 墳丘長：84メートル * 後方部 ** 長さ：48メートル ** 幅：48メートル ** 高さ：9.4メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：36メートル ** 幅：36メートル ** 高さ：5メートル File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む ファイル:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|有段口辺壺
{{small|大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館展示。}} ファイル:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|土師器
{{small|大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館展示。}} == 侍塚古墳群 == [[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|侍塚古墳群}}{{small|7号墳上から、左に5・6・8号墳、右奥に1・4号墳を望む。左最奥は下侍塚古墳。}}]] '''侍塚古墳群'''（さむらいづかこふんぐん）は、下侍塚古墳の北西にある[[古墳群]]。かつては10基ほど存在したが戦後の開田等で消滅し侍塚古墳群説明板。、現存する8基が大田原市指定史跡に指定されている。 [[前方後円墳]]1基・[[円墳]]6基・[[方墳]]1基の計8基から構成され、5・8号墳では部分的な発掘調査が実施されている。各古墳の内容は次の通り。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+侍塚古墳群の一覧 !古墳名 !画像 !形状 !規模 !出土品 !築造時期 !調査 |- |'''1号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長35-50m||[[円筒埴輪]]|| ||1999年測量調査 |- |'''2号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||[[円墳]]||直径17.5m|| || ||2001年測量調査 |- |'''3号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||円墳||直径18m|| || ||2000年測量調査 |- |'''4号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-4, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||円墳||直径16m|| || ||2000年測量調査 |- |'''5号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-5, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||円墳||直径27m||円筒埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]・
[[土師器]]・[[須恵器]]||[[6世紀]]代||1954年発掘調査 |- |'''6号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-6, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||円墳||直径12m|| || ||2001年測量調査 |- |'''7号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-7, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||円墳||直径15m|| || ||1998年測量調査 |- |'''8号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-8, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||[[方墳]]||一辺17m||土師器||下侍塚古墳と近い時期||1977年発掘調査 |} ファイル:侍塚1号墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|1号墳 円筒埴輪
{{small|大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 侍塚古墳 - 上侍塚古墳・下侍塚古墳を包括。1951年（昭和26年）6月9日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|483|侍塚古墳}}。 === 大田原市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 侍塚古墳群1号墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）2月15日指定[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ 大田原市の文化財一覧表]（大田原市ホームページ）。。 ** 侍塚古墳群2号墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）2月15日指定。 ** 侍塚古墳群3号墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）2月15日指定。 ** 侍塚古墳群4号墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）2月15日指定。 ** 侍塚古墳群5号墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）2月15日指定。 ** 侍塚古墳群6号墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）2月15日指定。 ** 侍塚古墳群7号墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）2月15日指定。 ** 侍塚古墳群8号墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）2月15日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館（大田原市湯津上） - 下侍塚古墳・侍塚古墳群の出土品等を保管・展示。旧栃木県立なす風土記の丘資料館湯津上館（2015年4月に栃木県より大田原市へ移管）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 下侍塚古墳説明板・侍塚古墳群説明板（大田原市教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=斎藤忠|authorlink=斎藤忠|chapter=侍塚古墳|year=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|侍塚古墳（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大金宣亮|year=|chapter=侍塚古墳|title=[[日本大百科全書]]（ニッポニカ）|publisher=[[小学館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|侍塚古墳（日本大百科全書）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=下侍塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|下侍塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=1989|chapter=下侍塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|下侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 侍塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|侍塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1976|chapter=|title= 下侍塚周濠発掘調査概報（湯津上村埋蔵文化財調査報告 第2集）|publisher=湯津上村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=眞保昌弘|year=2008|chapter=|title=侍塚古墳と那須国造碑 -下野の前方後方墳と古代石碑-（日本の遺跡25）|publisher=[[同成社]]|isbn=9784886214164|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[侍塚古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|483|侍塚古墳}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ 侍塚古墳]、[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082771179/ 侍塚古墳群] - 大田原市ホームページ {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=侍塚古墳群 |1-1=栃木県の古墳 |1-2=大田原市の歴史 |1-3=古墳群 |1-4=栃木県内の市町村指定史跡 }} {{デフォルトソート:しもさむらいつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:大田原市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]]

墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後方部） |別名 = 車塚 |所属 = |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[大田原市]]湯津上 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 46.75 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 7|経度秒 = 22.35 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長84m
高さ9.4m（後方部） |埋葬施設 = [[粘土槨]] |出土品 = 銅鏡・鉄製品・土師器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「[[侍塚古墳]]」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後方墳-南 |ラベル = 下侍塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''下侍塚古墳'''（しもさむらいづかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[大田原市]]湯津上（ゆづかみ）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[上侍塚古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」）。 上侍塚古墳とともに[[江戸時代]]に[[徳川光圀]]（[[水戸黄門]]）の命で日本で最初の学術的発掘調査が行われた古墳として知られる。本項では、下侍塚古墳の北西にある'''[[#侍塚古墳群|侍塚古墳群]]'''（大田原市指定史跡）についても解説する。 == 概要 ==

小川]]||colspan=2| ||[[駒形大塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||無||木炭槨 |- |rowspan=2|吉田

栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された古墳である。[[江戸時代]]の[[元禄]]5年（[[1692年]]）の発掘調査のほか、[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘長は84メートルを測り、那須地方の前方後方墳6基のうちでは上侍塚古墳に次ぐ規模になる。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められる{{Sfn|下侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされ、周濠内からは底部穿孔の土師器（赤彩の有段口辺壺）が検出されている{{Sfn|下侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における木棺の直葬で、両小口には粘土塊が置かれる{{Sfn|下侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。副葬品として、江戸期の調査では銅鏡（二神二獣鏡1）・鉄製品（刀片15・甲冑片・大刀把頭1・鉄鉾身1）・土師器（高坏4・壺か1）が検出されている{{Sfn|下侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末葉頃と推定される。上侍塚古墳とともに那須地方を代表する古墳の1つであるとともに、江戸期の調査後には上侍塚古墳と合わせて墳丘保存のための松が植えられていることから、日本で最初の学術的発掘調査かつ調査後の文化財保護の点で考古学史上においても重要視される古墳である。 古墳域は[[1951年]]（昭和26年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]、[[元禄]]5年（[[1692年]]）に[[徳川光圀]]の命で大金重貞による発掘調査。副葬品多数出土（『湯津神村車塚御修理』として記録、調査後は松板箱に入れて埋戻し）。 * [[1951年]]（昭和26年）6月9日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」のうち）。 * [[1975年]]（昭和50年）、周溝の範囲確認調査（湯津上村教育委員会、[[1976年]]に概報刊行）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}{{small|左に前方部、右に後方部。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り下侍塚古墳説明板。[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ 侍塚古墳]（大田原市ホームページ）。。 * 墳丘長：84メートル * 後方部 ** 長さ：48メートル ** 幅：48メートル ** 高さ：9.4メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：36メートル ** 幅：36メートル ** 高さ：5メートル File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む ファイル:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|有段口辺壺

{{small|大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館展示。}} == 侍塚古墳群 == [[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|侍塚古墳群}}{{small|7号墳上から、左に5・6・8号墳、右奥に1・4号墳を望む。左最奥は下侍塚古墳。}}]] '''侍塚古墳群'''（さむらいづかこふんぐん）は、下侍塚古墳の北西にある[[古墳群]]。かつては10基ほど存在したが戦後の開田等で消滅し侍塚古墳群説明板。、現存する8基が大田原市指定史跡に指定されている。 [[前方後円墳]]1基・[[円墳]]6基・[[方墳]]1基の計8基から構成され、5・8号墳では部分的な発掘調査が実施されている。各古墳の内容は次の通り。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+侍塚古墳群の一覧 !古墳名 !画像 !形状 !規模 !出土品 !築造時期 !調査 |- |'''1号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長35-50m||[[円筒埴輪]]|| ||1999年測量調査 |- |'''2号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||[[円墳]]||直径17.5m|| || ||2001年測量調査 |- |'''3号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||円墳||直径18m|| || ||2000年測量調査 |- |'''4号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-4, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||円墳||直径16m|| || ||2000年測量調査 |- |'''5号墳'''||[[File:Samuraizuka Kofun Group-5, funkyu.jpg|100px]]||円墳||直径27m||円筒埴輪・[[形象埴輪]]・

{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōtawara]] [[Category:Anterior-posterior Kofuns]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shimousamurai Kofun]] [[Category:Shimo-Samurai-Tsuka Kofun]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimousamurai Kofun]] [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[en:Shimousamurai Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shimousamurai Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|下侍塚古墳|version=99994858|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtawara|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtawara|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|下侍塚古墳|version=99994858|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōtawara]] [[Category:Anterior-posterior Kofuns]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shimousamurai Kofun]] [[Category:Shimo-Samurai-Tsuka Kofun]] }} [[sn:Shimousamurai Kofun]] [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[en:Shimousamurai Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtawara|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}} | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|ja|大田原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtawara|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun|ja|侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery

The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery

The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery

The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery

Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==

Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|下侍塚古墳|version=99994858|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Shimousamurai Kofun]] [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Otawara City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]]

Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
