Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|下侍塚古墳|version=99994858|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Shimousamurai Kofun]] [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Otawara City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==

Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|下侍塚古墳|version=99994858|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Shimousamurai Kofun]] [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Otawara City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shimousamurai Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|下侍塚古墳|version=99994858|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Shimousamurai Kofun]] [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Otawara City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Shimousamurai Kofun]]

Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|下侍塚古墳|version=99994858|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Otawara City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11360620}}

Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar

Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|下侍塚古墳|version=99994858|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Otawara City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11360620}}

Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar

The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. {{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|下侍塚古墳|version=99994858|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Otawara City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11360620}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:下侍塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.813197|N|140.122825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11360620]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar

Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} File:下侍塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Haji pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Yuzukami Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Samuraizuka Kofun (includes Shimosamuraizuka and Kamisamuraizuka Kofun) – Designated on June 9, 1951. === Municipal Historic Sites === * Samuraizuka Kofun Group – Designated on February 15, 1966.[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082775061/ Municipal Historic Sites List, Ōtawara City]. == Related Facilities == * Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimosamuraizuka Kofun and the Samuraizuka Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=2013|chapter=|title=The Story of Nasu Fudoki – Exploring the Ancient Kofuns of Nasu|publisher=Ōtawara City Nasu Fudoki-no-Oka Museum}} == See Also == * [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] – Ōtawara City * {{Commonscat|Shimosamuraizuka Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|下侍塚古墳|version=99994858|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Otawara City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11360620}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.813197|N|140.122825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11360620]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==

[[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] [[ja:下侍塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.813197|N|140.122825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11360620]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun | native_name = 下侍塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground: anterior section, background: posterior section) | other_name = Kurumazuka | location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | materials = {{ill|Clay-coffin burial|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay-coffin burial|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} | excavation = 1692 (Edo period), 1975 | designation1 = National Historic Site of Japan | designation1_date = 1951 }} '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' (下侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name [[:en:Samuraizuka Kofun|Samuraizuka Kofun]]. The tumulus is notable as one of the first Kofuns to be subjected to an academic archaeological investigation in Japan, conducted during the Edo period under the orders of Tokugawa Mitsukuni (Mito Kōmon). This article also covers the {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}, a collection of tumuli near Shimosamuraizuka Kofun that is designated as a municipal historic site. It is known as "the most beautiful kofun in Japan"{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}, ''Excavation of Kofun,'' Chūō Kōron Shinsho, 1965.. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Major Anterior-Posterior Kofuns in the Nasu Region ! colspan=3 | Kofun Groups ! Kofun Name ! Size ! Anterior Orientation ! Paving Stones ! Burial Method |- | rowspan=3 colspan=3 | || '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun''' || 84 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}} || 114 m || South || Yes || Clay-coffin burial |- | {{ill|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|lt=Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja|上侍塚北古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上侍塚古墳}} || 48.5 m || South || None || Unknown |}
Shimosamuraizuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Naka River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. In addition to the Edo period excavation in 1692, another investigation was conducted in 1975. The tumulus is an anterior-posterior Kofun with the anterior section oriented to the south. Measuring 84 meters in length, it is the second largest of the six anterior-posterior Kofuns in the Nasu region. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as a red-painted earthenware jar with a perforated base have been discovered.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtawara City|year=1988|chapter=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490093}} The burial chamber, located at the top of the posterior section, is a simple clay-coffin burial with clay blocks placed at both ends. Artifacts recovered during the Edo period excavation include bronze mirrors (a Two Gods and Two Beasts Mirror), iron implements (15 sword fragments, armor pieces, a sword hilt, and a spearhead), and earthenware (four tall cups and a jar). The tumulus dates to the end of the 4th century, during the early Middle Kofun period. Following the Edo period investigation, pine trees were planted to protect the mound, making Shimosamuraizuka Kofun one of the earliest examples of cultural heritage preservation in Japan. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" designation.{{Cite web|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/|publisher=National Cultural Property Database}} == Mound Details == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun Information Board. * Total length: 84 meters * Posterior section: ** Length: 48 meters ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 9.4 meters * Anterior section: ** Length: 36 meters ** Width: 36 meters ** Height: 5 meters == Samuraizuka Kofun Group == {{ill|Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja|侍塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samuraizuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下侍塚古墳|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}} is a collection of Kofuns located northwest of Shimosamuraizuka Kofun. While around 10 mounds originally existed, only eight remain today and have been designated as municipal historic sites. The group consists of one anterior-posterior Kofun, six round mounds, and one square mound. Partial excavations have been conducted on mounds 5 and 8. File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Posterior section mound top File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the anterior section toward the posterior section File:Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the posterior section toward the anterior section File:下侍塚古墳出土 有段口辺壺.JPG|Stepped rim jar
